seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Codex is now fully open source; users can inspect its codebase, ask it to explain features, optimize setups, and observe how the team builds with it. Key pattern: turn repeated workflows into reusable skills or delegate to custom subagents.

Prompt Codex to analyze past sessions for repeated tasks (e.g., CI fixes, PR reviews, changelogs, debugging) and auto-generate reusable skills for workflows or bounded subagents for specific jobs; improves long-horizon goal handling.
Extremely cheap (~100x cheaper) model optimized for small tasks and operating on large volumes of data/records; strong practical efficiency for high-volume lightweight inference.

global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Massive open-source repo (~435 lessons, 20 phases, 320 hours) teaching full AI engineering by building everything from scratch: backprop, tokenizers, transformers, agent loops, memory systems, RLHF, swarms, MCP servers, production infra. Ships reusable artifacts at every step; Python/TS/Rust/Julia.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Telegram-based persistent AI agent with memory for workflows, skills, and local data; turns repetitive tasks into fast commands.
near_misses.reason: Promotional content focused on cost savings rather than novel technical architecture or benchmarks.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Autonomous agent system for social media deployment, content creation, and posting.
near_misses.reason: Marketing/demo focused; lacks deep technical details on agent orchestration or memory.

notes: Seed experts yielded strong signals around OpenAI Codex openness and usage patterns plus one efficiency model. Global search surfaced one standout educational repo; most other results were promotional or tangential. No posts from @karpathy in the window. Limited high-signal volume overall.
